B1 standard physics + Chapman-Sq with NASA Besselian-elements anchors (path width 294.0 km ±5 km, max central-line duration 138.2 s ±0.1 s, Hartland 88% obscuration ±2 pp; Chapman amplitude 5–25 nT high-coverage quiet conditions). B2 dome-geometry-forced from V51.1 published params (h_Sun=5733, h_Moon=2534, FORM-V51-001/002/004/005, eclipse formula ΔB=baseline×coverage×FSF, per-station ΔB at BOU baseline -10.9 nT yields range -5.04 to -12.83 nT, totality circle 1.317 km diameter, penumbra band 84.7 km, single-station totality 0.9–7 s, 0/9 dome-cited stations inside dome-geometry penumbra band). B3 symmetric falsification: dome-falsifying observations (NASA-style path matches 294 km), standard-physics-falsifying observations (~1 km path width matches dome), our-analysis-falsifying observations (both buckets simultaneously hold OR unanticipated mechanism).
